Login Format (Username)

Please enter your

(username alone is not sufficient).

Quick Setup (Recommended)

Use the

eduroam CAT

installer to automatically configure eduroam for your device.

Windows Setup

macOS Setup

Android Setup

iOS/iPadOS Profile

If CAT is not available, you can follow the "Manual Setup" steps below.

Windows 10/11 – Manual Setup

1

Select

eduroam

from the network list.

2

Enter your username (full email) and KHAS password.

3

If advanced settings are required:

EAP method:

PEAP

Inner authentication:

MSCHAPv2

CA Certificate / Server:

Approve the corporate certificate.

4

Connect

→ Internet access becomes active upon verification.

Android – Manual Setup

EAP method:

PEAP

Phase 2 authentication:

MSCHAPv2

CA Certificate:

"Use system certificates" (or "Validate" depending on device). Consult IT Support if unsure.

Identity:

full email (format as above)

Anonymous identity:

can be left empty

Password:

your KHAS password

Menu names may vary by manufacturer.

iOS / iPadOS

Select

eduroam

from the network list, connect with your full email and password. If a certificate warning appears, select

Trust

.

Setup via

is recommended if possible.

eduroam at Other Campuses

Your device connects automatically at eduroam member institutions;

your username must always include the KHAS domain

.

Connection speed and access policies may vary by the host institution.

For your security, do not save your password on public computers; keep your device updated.

Your usage is subject to the university's network and information security policies.

Frequently Asked Questions

I cannot connect / I get an authentication error.

First, ensure you type your username in

full email

format.
                  Reset your password if it is not current. On Android,

must be selected; on Windows,

.

eduroam does not appear in the list.

Toggle Wi-Fi off and on, enable airplane mode briefly and turn it off. If the issue persists, you might be out of range.

I changed my password, the connection was lost.

"Forget" the eduroam network on your device and reconnect with your new password. Update the auto-connect option.
